nodes.name: Olamide
nodes.blurb: The spine of the archive — Bariga upstart, YBNL founder, street-hop architect, label boss raising the next wave.

nodes.name: ID Cabasa
nodes.blurb: Coded Tunes founder and early producer — signed a young Yoruba rapper when the industry still said English.

nodes.name: Yoruba street rap
nodes.blurb: The linguistic and cultural current Cabasa's scene rode — indigenous bars as commercial risk, then blueprint.

nodes.name: Empire
nodes.blurb: YBNL's 2020 joint-venture distribution partner — the deal that opened the roster, including Fireboy DML, to international reach.

nodes.name: Phyno
nodes.blurb: The Igbo-language counterpart across eras — from 2 Kings through UY Scuti, the constant booth partner.

nodes.name: Wizkid
nodes.blurb: From Omo To Shan on Rapsodi to Kai! on the self-titled — two Lagos careers orbiting each other for over a decade.

nodes.name: Reminisce
nodes.blurb: Fellow street-hop architect of the 2010s Lagos circuit — shared stages and the same mainland-to-mainstream fight.

nodes.name: Pheelz
nodes.blurb: Long-time in-house producer and YBNL alumnus — from early Rapsodi sessions to later reunion cuts.

nodes.name: Young Jonn
nodes.blurb: Producer behind Bobo and later alumni reunions — the beatmaker who soundtracked the Street King peak.

nodes.name: Burna Boy
nodes.blurb: Guest on Omo Wobe Anthem (2016) — two futures of Nigerian music sharing a YBNL beat in the Street King years.

nodes.name: Omah Lay
nodes.blurb: Infinity — the reinvention-era co-star who helped prove street-hop's founder could lead with melody.

nodes.name: Wande Coal
nodes.blurb: Afrobeats melody on the Who You Epp? Refix and The Glory sessions — street-hop borrowing pop's shine.

nodes.name: Lil Kesh
nodes.blurb: Early YBNL signee — Shoki-era breakout and proof the label could mint stars beyond its founder.

nodes.name: Adekunle Gold
nodes.blurb: Designed YBNL's logo before Olamide signed him in 2015 — debut album Gold reached the Billboard World Albums chart before he left to go solo in 2016.

nodes.name: Fireboy DML
nodes.blurb: YBNL's melodic second generation — Empire-era reach, Carpe Diem closer, and the label's international face.

nodes.name: Asake
nodes.blurb: The log-drum breakout Olamide signed and then traded bars with — Unruly and Ikigai as living proof of the pipeline, before launching his own imprint, Giran Republic, in 2025.

nodes.name: Bnxn
nodes.blurb: On Unruly with the label's second generation — another ear Olamide backed as the founder stepped into elder mode.
